questions.prompt: Where should the real auth check happen — middleware or layout/page?

choices.text: Both, with different jobs. Middleware does the cheap check (does the cookie exist? Is the JWT signature valid? Is it expired?) on every matching request — fast, runs on Edge, redirects unauthorized requests early. Layouts and Server Actions do the full check (load the user from DB, check status, RBAC, organization membership) — slower but only when needed. Database lookups in middleware are slow and run on every request including assets. Server Actions ALWAYS verify on their own — never trust UI-level gating; anyone can call the action directly.

questions.explanation: Middleware = cheap cookie/JWT check. Layout/Page/Action = full DB-backed check.

questions.prompt: User can only delete their own posts. UI hides the delete button for others. Is that enough?

choices.text: No — UI-only gating is purely cosmetic. An attacker can call the Server Action directly (browser devtools, scripts, curl) and bypass the hidden button. The Server Action MUST verify on its own: `const session = await getSession(); if (!session) throw; const post = await db.posts.findUnique(...); if (post.authorId !== session.user.id) throw 'Forbidden';`. The server is the security boundary. UI gating is good UX (don't show buttons that won't work) but doesn't substitute for server-side authorization.

questions.explanation: UI gating is cosmetic. Server Actions must verify auth + authorization themselves.
